volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 2.1.477b - Section 3.2.578)

sections.label: Section 2.1.477b
sections.original: Dicam . viscum legioni dedi
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . I gave birdlime and slings to my troops ; beneath it they laid leaves of coltsfoot [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.1.479
sections.original: Quoi rei ?
sections.translation: For what purpose ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.479b
sections.original: Ad fundas viscus né adhaeresceret .
sections.translation: That the birdlime mightn ’ t adhere to the slings .

sections.label: Section 2.1.480
sections.original: Perge . optume hercle perieras . quid postea ?
sections.translation: Proceed . ( Aside .) I faith , you do lie most egregiously . ( Aloud .) What after that ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.481
sections.original: In fundas visci indebant grandiculos globos ,
sections.translation: They placed pretty large pellets of birdlime in their slings : with which I ordered them to be taken aim at as they flew . Why many words ? Each one did they hit with the birdlime —

sections.label: Section 2.1.484_485
sections.original: tam crebri ad terram áccidebant quam pira .
sections.translation: they fell to the ground as thick as pears . As each one dropped , I straightway pierced him through the brain with his own feathers , just like a turtle-dove .

sections.label: Section 2.1.488
sections.original: Si hercle istuc umquam factum est , tum me Iuppiter
sections.translation: By my troth , if ever this did take place , then may Jupiter make me to be ever sacrificing , and never propitiating him .

sections.label: Section 2.1.490
sections.original: An mi haec non credis ?
sections.translation: And don ’ t you . believe me in this ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.490b
sections.original: Credo , ut mi aequomst credier .
sections.translation: I do believe , in the same degree that it is proper that I should be believed . Come , let ’ s go in-doors , until the entrails are brought home .

sections.label: Section 2.1.491b
sections.original: Dum exta referuntur , volo
sections.translation: I wish to relate to you a single battle more .

sections.label: Section 2.1.492b
sections.original: Nil moror .
sections.translation: I don ’ t care about it .

sections.label: Section 2.1.493
sections.original: Ausculta .
sections.translation: Do listen .

sections.label: Section 2.1.493b
sections.original: Non hercle auscultabo .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , no .

sections.label: Section 2.1.493c
sections.original: Quo modo ?
sections.translation: Why then I ’ ll break your head this instant , if you don ’ t listen ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.495
sections.original: nisi aut auscultas aut is in malam crucem .
sections.translation: or else be off to utter perdition !

sections.label: Section 2.1.496
sections.original: Malam crucem ibo potius .
sections.translation: I ’ d sooner go to utter perdition !

sections.label: Section 2.1.496b
sections.original: Certumnest tibi ?
sections.translation: Are you determined then ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.497
sections.original: Certum .
sections.translation: Determined .

sections.label: Section 2.1.497b
sections.original: Tum tu igitur die bonó Aphrodisiis
sections.translation: In that case , do you , then , upon this lucky day , the Aphrodisia , make over to me the younger one of your courtesans .

sections.label: Section 2.1.499
sections.original: Ita res divina mihi fuit : res serias
sections.translation: The sacred ceremony has by its omens been to me today of such a nature — I put off all serious matters from today until another day .

sections.label: Section 2.1.501
sections.original: Profestos festos habeam decretum est mihi .
sections.translation: I am resolved to make it really a holiday .

sections.label: Section 2.1.502
sections.original: Nunc hinc eamus intro . sequere hac me .—
sections.translation: Now let ’ s go hence in-doors . Follow me this way .

sections.label: Section 2.1.502b
sections.original: Seqor .
sections.translation: I follow . For this day , then , I ’ m out on hire to you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.504
sections.original: Íta me di ament , tardo amico nihil est quicquam inaequius ,
sections.translation: So may the Deities love me , there ’ s nothing more annoying than a tardy friend ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.505
sections.original: praesertim homini amanti , qui quidquid agit properat omnia .
sections.translation: especially to a man in love , who ’ s in a hurry in everything that he does ; just as I ’ m leading on these assistants , fellows of most crawling step ; they are more slow than merchant-ships in a calm sea . And upon my faith , I really did on purpose wave my aged friends ; I knew they were too slow through their years ; I apprehended delay to my passion ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.510
sections.original: nequiquam hos procos mi elegi loripedis , tardissimos .
sections.translation: in vain I selected for myself these young fellows on their preferment , timber-legged , most tardy chaps . Well ( turning round to them .) if you are going to come to-day , get on , or get off hence to utter perdition ! Is this the way it befits friends to give their assistance to a person in love ? Why sure , this pace was bolted through a fine floursieve [*] ; unless you have been practising in fetters to creep along thus with this step .

sections.label: Section 3.1.515
sections.original: Heus tu , quamquam nos videmur tibi plebeii et pauperes ,
sections.translation: Hark you ! although we seem to you of the commonalty and poor , if you don ’ t speak us fair , you rich man of highest rank , we are in the habit of boldly playing the mischief with the rich man ; we are under no engagement to you , about what it is that you love or hate . When we paid money for our freedom [*] , we paid our own , not yours ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.520
sections.original: liberos nos esse oportet . nos te nihili pendimus ,
sections.translation: it ’ s right that we should be under no restraint . We value you at nought ; don ’ t you fancy that we ’ ve been made over as slaves to your passion . It ’ s proper for free men to go through the city at a moderate pace ; I deem it like a slave to be running along in a bustle . Especially when the state is at peace and the enemies are slain ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.525
sections.original: non decet tumultuari . sed si properabas magis ,
sections.translation: it is not decent to make a tumult . But if you were for making greater haste , you ought to have brought us here as assistants the day before . Don ’ t you fancy it — not any one of us will this day be running through the streets , nor yet shall the people pelt us with stones for madmen .

sections.label: Section 3.1.529
sections.original: At si ad prandium me in aedem vos dixissem ducere ,
sections.translation: But if I had said that I was taking you to a Temple [*] to breakfast ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.530
sections.original: vinceretis cervom cursu vel gralatorem gradu ;
sections.translation: you would have surpassed a stag in speed , or a man on stilts in your steps . Now , because I have said that I am taking you as my assistants and witnesses , you are gouty , and in the slowness of your pace have been outdoing the snail .

sections.label: Section 3.1.533
sections.original: An vero non iusta causa est , quor curratur celeriter
sections.translation: Why , really , is there not good cause for running swiftly , where you are to drink and eat at another man ’ s expense as much as you please , until you are full ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.535
sections.original: quod tu invitus numquam reddas domino , de quoio ederis ?
sections.translation: what you need never return against your will to your host , at whose expense you have been eating ? But still , in some way or other , although we are poor men , we have at home something to eat ; don ’ t you browbeat us in such a contemptuous way . Whatever that very little is , that little of ours is all at home ; we neither dun any one ourselves , nor does any one dun us .

sections.label: Section 3.1.540
sections.original: tua causa nemo nostrorumst suos rupturus ramites .
sections.translation: Not one of us is going to burst the veins of his lungs for your sake .

sections.label: Section 3.1.541
sections.original: Nimis iracundi estis : equidem haec vobis dixi per iocum .
sections.translation: You are too warm ; really , I said this to you in joke .

sections.label: Section 3.1.542
sections.original: Per iocum itidem dictum habeto quae nos tibi respondimus .
sections.translation: Consider it said in joke as well what we have said to you in answer .

sections.label: Section 3.1.543
sections.original: Obsecro hercle , operam celocem hanc mihi , ne corbitam date ;
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee , do give me this aid of yours like a fly-boat , not a merchant-ship . Do hobble along at least , for I do not ask you to hurry .

sections.label: Section 3.1.545
sections.original: Si quid tu placide otioseque agere vis , operam damus ;
sections.translation: If you wish to do anything quietly and leisurely , we lend our aid ; if you are in a hurry , it would be better for you to hire runners [*] as your assistants .

sections.label: Section 3.1.547
sections.original: Scitis rem , narravi vobis quod vestra opera mi opus siet ,
sections.translation: You understand ( the matter I ’ ve informed you of ), that I have need of your assistance with regard to this Procurer , who has so long trifled with me in my amour ; that a scheme is to be planned against him about the gold and . my servant .

sections.label: Section 3.1.550
sections.original: Omnia istaec scimus iam nos , si hi spectatores sciant ;
sections.translation: All that we know already , if these Spectators know . For the sake of these Spectators it is that this Play is now being acted . ’ Twere better for you to inform them , that when you do anything , they may know what it is you are doing . Don ’ t you trouble about us ; we know the whole matter ; since we all learnt it together in company with yourself , so that we can answer you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.555
sections.original: Ita profecto est . sed agite igitur , ut sciam vos scire rem ,
sections.translation: Such really is the fact ; but come , that I may be sure then that you know it , repeat the matter at length , and tell me what I told you just now .

sections.label: Section 3.1.557
sections.original: Itane ? temptas an sciamus ? non meminisse nos ratu'sratus es ,
sections.translation: Are you trying in this way whether we know ? Do you suppose we don ’ t remember how you have given three hundred Philippeans to Collybiscus your bailiff , for him to bring here to the Procurer , your enemy ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.560
sections.original: isque sé ut assimularet peregrinum esse aliunde ex alio oppido ?
sections.translation: and to pretend that he is a foreigner from a distance , from another city ? When he has brought them , you ’ ll go there to seek your servant together with the money .

sections.label: Section 3.1.562b
sections.original: Meministis memoriter , servastis me .
sections.translation: You remember it by heart ; you have saved me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.563
sections.original: Ille negabit : Milphionem quaeri censebit tuom ;
sections.translation: He ’ ll be for denying it ; he ’ ll suppose your Milphio is being looked for . He ’ ll have to pay double all the money stolen ; the Procurer will be adjudged to you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.565
sections.original: ad eam rem nos esse testis vis tibi .
sections.translation: In this matter you wish us to be your witnesses .

sections.label: Section 3.1.565b
sections.original: Tenetis rem .
sections.translation: You ’ ve got the matter fast .

sections.label: Section 3.1.566
sections.original: Vix quidem hercle , íta pauxilla est , digitulis primoribus .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , hardly with the tips [*] of our fingers , indeed ; it is so very small a one .

sections.label: Section 3.1.567
sections.original: Hoc cito et cursim est agendum . propera iam quantum potest .
sections.translation: This must be done quickly and with expedition . Make as much haste , then , as you can .

sections.label: Section 3.1.568
sections.original: Bene vale igitur . te advocatos meliust celeris ducere ;
sections.translation: Kindly fare you well , then ; it ’ s better for you to provide some active assistants , we are but slow ones .

sections.label: Section 3.1.569b
sections.original: Optime itis , pessime hercle dicitis .
sections.translation: You move very well [*] . ( Aside .) But very badly do you speak me , faith .

sections.label: Section 3.1.570
sections.original: quin etiam deciderint vobis femina ín talos velim .
sections.translation: ( Aloud .) Moreover , I could wish your thighs to fall down into your ankles .

sections.label: Section 3.1.571
sections.original: At edepol nos tibi in lumbos linguam atque oculos in solum .
sections.translation: And , i ’ faith , we that your tongue had fallen into your loins , and your eyes upon the ground .

sections.label: Section 3.1.572
sections.original: Heia , hau vostrumst iracundos esse , quod dixi ioco .
sections.translation: Heyday ! it ’ s not for you to be angry at what I said in joke .

sections.label: Section 3.1.573
sections.original: Nec tuom quidem est amicis per iocum iniuste loqui .
sections.translation: Nor for you , indeed , to be speaking ill to your friends in joke .

sections.label: Section 3.1.574
sections.original: Mittite istaec . quid velim vos , scitis .
sections.translation: Drop this . What I want to do , you understand .

sections.label: Section 3.1.574b
sections.original: Callemus probe :
sections.translation: We know full well :

sections.label: Section 3.1.575
sections.original: lenonem ut periurum perdas , id studes .
sections.translation: to undo the perjured Procurer , it ’ s that you wish .

sections.label: Section 3.1.575b
sections.original: Tenetis rem .
sections.translation: You ’ ve got the matter right . See , Milphio and the bailiff are opportunely coming out together . He ’ s coming rigged out like a nobleman , and appropriately , for the plot .

sections.label: Section 3.2.578
sections.original: Iam tenes praecepta in corde ?
sections.translation: Have you now got your instructions by heart ?
